summary: Matched a small but high-value slice of `src/melee/gm/gm_1A36.c`, adding game-mode scene-management code and supporting declarations across library, VI, debug, trophy, DVD, audio, MTHP, card/game, and un headers. The PR converted seven scratch entries around `0x801A3680` through `0x801BF9A8` to `OK`, with reported full matches for `gm_801A4310`, `gm_801A43A0`, `gm_801A4510`, and `gm_801BF9A8`, while `gm_801A4014` remained near but not perfect in the report excerpt. There were no review comments in the provided slice.
what_changed: Added substantial C implementation scaffolding to `gm_1A36.c`, including local `MinorScene`, `MajorScene`, `MinorSceneHandler`, `SceneNums`, and `GameState` types.
Defined static `GameState gm_80479D30` and asserted `sizeof(GameState) == 0x14`, documenting and enforcing the expected game-state layout.
Implemented or exposed scene-related functions including `gm_801A4014`, `gm_801A4310`, `gm_801A43A0`, `gm_801A4510`, and `gm_801BF9A8` in the matched slice.
Added inline helper routines `matchMinor` and `findSceneMatching` to express minor-scene and major-scene table searches while preserving matching behavior.

Added game-loop logic in `gm_801A4510`, including major-scene initialization, progressive/DTV startup scene selection via `VIGetDTVStatus`, `db_804D6B30`, and `OSGetProgressiveMode`, and repeated dispatch through `gm_801A43A0`.

Added `gm_801BF9A8` logic for Pikmin trophy unlock handling using `un_803048C0`, `lb_8001C2D8`, `un_803124BC`, and `Trophy_SetUnlockState`, then setting a scene exit callback and entering the next major scene.
Updated `config/GALE01/scratches.txt` so scratch entries for `func_801A3680`, `gm_801A4014`, `func_801A4310`, `func_801A43A0`, `func_801A4510`, and `gm_801BF9A8` are marked `OK`.
Added missing prototypes and externs needed by the new gm code, such as `VIGetDTVStatus`, `db_804D6B30`, `lbAudioAx_80027DBC`, `lbDvd_80018F58`, `lbMthp_8001F800`, and several `lbcardgame` functions.
Changed `lb_8000C160`'s size parameter from `int` to `ssize_t` and included `<stddef.h>` in `lb_00B0.h`.
Reorganized `un_2FC9.h` with address comments, address-sorted declarations, and a GALE-address-style include guard.
Created a proper include guard and trophy unlock declaration in `src/melee/ty/toy.h`.
smart_moves: Kept uncertain or file-local layouts inside `gm_1A36.c` rather than prematurely promoting all scene structs to public headers.
Used `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(GameState) == 0x14)` to lock down a critical state struct size before relying on it for `lb_8000C160` zeroing and global-state access.

Introduced narrowly scoped inline helpers for table searches, but preserved a more manual control-flow block under `#if 0` in `gm_801A4014` where the compiler-sensitive version was needed for matching.
Added only the external prototypes required to support the matched implementation instead of broad header rewrites.
Marked scratch entries as `OK` after integrating their code into the source tree, making future scratch searches cleaner.
Captured known behavioral context with comments such as `UnclePunch: Scene_Main`, `Set scene exit callback?`, and the Gekko boot-to-CSS note without over-renaming unknown pieces.
decomp_lessons: For scene-manager code, model table entries as small local structs first, then enforce known sizes with static assertions when global state is manipulated directly.

Compiler-sensitive matching may require keeping explicit loop/goto structure instead of using the cleaner helper expression, as shown by the disabled `matchMinor` assignment versus the active manual loop in `gm_801A4014`.

When matching code touches many subsystems, the supporting work is often mostly header hygiene: add precise prototypes and externs for DVD, VI, audio, debug globals, card/game helpers, MTHP, trophy, and un functions.
Scratch entries should be updated from percentages to `OK` when their implementations land, even if the overall file percentage remains low.
Do not overinterpret unknown scene callback fields; names like `unk_struct_0`, `unk_struct_1`, and `unk_func` were retained where evidence was weak.
Address comments in headers, as added/reordered in `un_2FC9.h`, improve searchability and make later matching work easier to correlate with symbols.
naming_conventions: Public matched functions use the `gm_801Axxxx` / `gm_801BF9A8` address-based convention when no semantic name is established.
Static forward declarations in `gm_1A36.c` include address comments such as `/* 1A3EF4 */ static void gm_801A3EF4(void);`.
Local scene structs use semantic names `MinorScene`, `MajorScene`, `MinorSceneHandler`, `SceneNums`, and `GameState` while preserving unknown fields with `unk_` names.
Callback fields are capitalized according to apparent role in tables: `Prep`, `Decide`, `Load`, `Unload`, `Init`, `OnFrame`, `OnLoad`, and `OnLeave`.
Unknown fields retain offset-like names such as `x0D`, `x0E`, `x0F`, and `x0`, `x4`, `x8` when their meaning is not proven.
Header guards in touched area use GALE address-style names, e.g. `GALE01_2FC9B4` and `GALE01_305058`.
Header declarations are annotated with address comments like `/* 01F800 */ void lbMthp_8001F800(void);`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used an explicit `#if 0` clean helper version beside an active hand-shaped loop/goto version in `gm_801A4014`, indicating the final control flow was chosen for codegen matching.
Preserved seemingly unused locals such as `u32 unused;` and `u64 unused;`, likely to influence stack/register allocation for matching.
Used casts to anonymous structs and raw pointer views, such as `((u8*) &gm_80479D30)[0]` in `gm_801A4310` and `(void*) var_r27` for scene data, to reproduce expected access patterns.
Used sentinel table scans with hardcoded values `0xFF` for minor scenes and `45` / `0x2D` for major scene termination.
Kept global accesses and temporary pointer variables such as `temp_r30 = &gmMainLib_8046B0F0.x4` instead of simplifying repeated conditions, likely to match register reuse.
Used ternary selection for language-dependent Pikmin data file IDs: `GPIJ` for Japanese and `GPIE` otherwise.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available; conclusions are based on title, report excerpt, changed files, and diff content only.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that promote `MinorScene`, `MajorScene`, `MinorSceneHandler`, or `GameState` from local `gm_1A36.c` definitions into shared headers.
Check subsequent work on `gm_801A4014` to see whether the partially matched report score was improved and whether the `#if 0` helper version became usable.
Search for later renames of `gm_801BF9A8` or documentation around the Pikmin trophy unlock and scene transition behavior.
Look for later cleanups of `gmMainLib_8046B0F0` fields `x0`, `x4`, and `x8`.
Investigate whether `VIGetDTVStatus` received a concrete return type after being introduced as `UNK_T`.
Search for additional `scratches.txt` entries around `gm_1A36` that remained percentage-based after this PR.
